title: Introduction to Computer Programming
notes: A common first programming course and a usual starting point for students with little or no coding background.
warnings: Do not assume it is easy just because it is introductory.
Check current prerequisites, exclusions, and program rules officially.

title: Introduction to Computer Science
notes: A key next programming course after CSC108 or equivalent background, with more abstract programming and data structure ideas.
warnings: This can be heavy if your programming foundation is shaky.
Verify current POSt and prerequisite rules before planning around it.

title: Mathematical Expression and Reasoning for Computer Science
notes: Proofs, logic, and mathematical reasoning are central. The workload can feel different from programming-heavy CS courses.
warnings: Students often underestimate the proof-writing adjustment.
Verify whether CSC165, CSC240, or another option fits your target program.

title: Enriched Introduction to the Theory of Computation
notes: An enriched theoretical CS option for students with strong mathematical maturity and interest in proofs.
warnings: This is not the default choice for most first-year students.
Confirm exclusions, prerequisites, and whether it fits your intended CS path officially.

title: Calculus and Linear Algebra for Commerce
notes: A quantitative course often associated with commerce-style planning rather than math specialist preparation.
warnings: Do not assume it substitutes for every calculus requirement.
Verify whether your target program expects MAT130, MAT135/136, MAT137, or another stream.

title: Calculus and Linear Algebra for Commerce
notes: A full-year calculus and linear algebra style option commonly associated with commerce/economics planning.
warnings: Program fit varies; verify with the Calendar and department pages.
Check exclusions before pairing it with other calculus courses.

title: Calculus I
notes: A common first calculus option. Pace and assessment style can still be a jump from high school.
warnings: Check whether your intended program requires MAT135/136, MAT137, MAT157, or a different calculus stream.

title: Calculus II
notes: Common continuation after MAT135, with integration and series-style topics depending on the offering.
warnings: Confirm prerequisites and whether the MAT135/136 stream satisfies your program requirements.

title: Calculus with Proofs
notes: A proof-oriented calculus course. It can be rewarding, but it is a major workload commitment for many first-year students.
warnings: Do not choose it only because it sounds stronger; check fit, background, and program needs.
Difficulty is highly subjective and instructor-dependent.

title: Analysis I
notes: An enriched proof-heavy analysis course usually suited to students with strong interest in rigorous mathematics.
warnings: This can dominate a first-year schedule.
Verify whether your intended path actually needs this level of rigor.

title: Linear Algebra I
notes: Introduces linear algebra ideas used across math, stats, economics, CS, and data-heavy paths.
warnings: Check whether your program expects MAT223, MAT240, or another linear algebra option.

title: Linear Algebra II
notes: A follow-up linear algebra course that is more advanced than MAT223 and often relevant to more theoretical quantitative paths.
warnings: Usually not a casual first-year add-on; verify prerequisites and timing carefully.

title: Algebra I
notes: An enriched abstract algebra/linear algebra style option for mathematically strong students.
warnings: Confirm whether MAT240 is appropriate versus MAT223 for your intended program.
Expect a proof-heavy workload.

title: Algebra II
notes: A continuation in enriched algebra/linear algebra for students pursuing a rigorous math sequence.
warnings: Verify sequencing and prerequisites officially before planning this in first year.

title: Introduction to Statistical Reasoning and Data Science
notes: A common first statistics course for students exploring data, statistics, or quantitative programs.
warnings: Verify current statistics program requirements and exclusions before relying on it.

title: Principles of Microeconomics
notes: A standard first economics course and common starting point for economics paths.
warnings: Program entry rules can be specific; verify grades, combinations, and exclusions officially.

title: Principles of Macroeconomics
notes: Common companion to ECO101 for students considering economics or related programs.
warnings: Check whether ECO101 and ECO102 are both needed for your intended program.

title: Introductory Psychology
notes: A broad introduction to psychology and a common course for students exploring psychology or cognitive science.
warnings: Large intro courses can be memorization-heavy.
Verify program requirements and exclusions officially.

title: Adaptation and Biodiversity
notes: A common first biology course focused on evolution, ecology, biodiversity, and biological thinking.

title: Molecular and Cell Biology
notes: A common first biology course focused on molecular and cellular biology foundations.
